fb: Too arrogant. Confidence is good, but this comes across as dismissive of others.
Perfect — humble, diplomatic, and still confident in your own work.
Too self-deprecating. You want to show confidence, not doubt.
Too blunt. Criticising the system publicly can create controversy.
Diplomatic and measured — acknowledges the question without causing drama.
Sounds dismissive and can come across as avoiding the question entirely.
Getting defensive can make you look guilty. Stay calm.
Great answer — charming, light, and confident without being defensive.
'No comment' makes it seem like you have something to hide.
Sounds overconfident and dismissive. A little vulnerability is relatable.
Honest and relatable — shows self-awareness and professionalism.
Too negative. This doesn't inspire confidence in your audience.
Confrontational and rude. This will not go down well internationally.
Elegant response — acknowledges the criticism and reframes it positively.
Don't undermine your own country's music. Be proud and constructive.
Blaming others publicly is never a good look. It makes you seem unprofessional.
Professional and composed. Shows resilience and grace under pressure.
If the issue was visible to viewers, pretending it didn't happen seems dishonest.
Very rude and will create negative headlines. Never dismiss other artists.
Diplomatic and positive — avoids picking favourites while showing respect.
Picking a clear favourite can offend other delegations and create drama.
Sounds mercenary. Eurovision isn't supposed to be about money.
Loyal to Sweden but open-minded — a classy answer.
A bit too rigid. Many beloved Eurovision artists have represented different countries.
Sore loser attitude. This will attract negative press.
Gracious and positive. Reframes the result in a way that emphasises the achievement.
Sounds dismissive and inauthentic. The audience knows you care.
Even if it's false, shutting it down this hard closes the door. Be more playful.
Perfect tease — builds excitement without confirming or denying.
If your label hasn't announced it yet, leaking details in an interview could cause problems.
